title: Internationalised numerals (>=1 additional numeral system)
context: For learners whose home numeral system is not Western Arabic (0-9), manipulatives labelled only in Western Arabic numerals present an additional cognitive load or may be inaccessible entirely. Eastern Arabic numerals (used across Arabic-speaking countries) and Devanagari numerals (used in Hindi-medium instruction) are among the most widely used alternatives. Adding support for at least one additional numeral system to the generator engine allows the same pedagogically verified manipulative to serve a broader population.
objective: Add internationalised numeral rendering support to the generator engine for at least one additional numeral system, with a math-educator and native-script reviewer sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: The generator engine supports at least one additional numeral system (e.g. Eastern Arabic or Devanagari) via a locale/numeral-system parameter in the spec.
All numeral glyphs in the output use only open-licensed fonts covering the target script; font license and attribution recorded.
Output PDFs pass the automated verification harness (dimensional correctness unchanged); numeral legibility at standard and large-print sizes confirmed by a native-script reviewer.
A math educator confirms the numeral representations are mathematically correct and appropriate for the target educational context.
The implementation is extensible: adding a second numeral system requires only a new font + glyph mapping, not architectural changes.
MIT-licensed; all commits DCO-signed; passes pnpm build, pnpm test, and CI gate.
resources: tasks/math-manip-code-001.json -- generator engine (numeral rendering to extend)
tasks/math-manip-research-001.json -- open-asset license clearance (must extend for new script fonts)
Unicode numeral system references for Eastern Arabic (U+0660-U+0669) and Devanagari (U+0966-U+096F)
output: Internationalised numeral support integrated into the generator engine, with at least one locale-specific spec example and output PDFs, in a pull request, MIT-licensed.
